title: ClosedOperator
summary: Builds the closed OR linear combination from its coefficient system and packages its stated properties.

mathematicalContent.heading: Mathematical role
mathematicalContent.body: Builds the closed OR linear combination from its coefficient system and packages its stated properties.

mathematicalContent.heading: How to read the translation
mathematicalContent.body: The correspondence below follows PolyGJMSoperators/OR/ClosedOperator.lean declaration by declaration. A definition is translated by its defining expression, a theorem by its quantified proposition, and a structure, class, or inductive type by the mathematical data encoded in its fields or constructors. The original Lean signature remains visible beside every translation.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: OR operator data using the regular closed coefficients. In explicit terms, for n in the natural numbers, and n, w₁, w₂ of type ℚ, its defining expression is N : equals N w₁ : equals w₁ w₂ : equals w₂ n : equals n coeff System : equals ORCoefficient System.regular N n w₁ w₂.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \operatorname{regular}(N,n,w₁,w₂)=N = N w₁ = w₁ w₂ = w₂ n = n \operatorname{coeffSystem} = \operatorname{ORCoefficientSystem}.\operatorname{regular} N n w₁ w₂
correspondence.leanType: def regular (N : ℕ) (n w₁ w₂ : ℚ) : OROperatorData

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For N in the natural numbers, n, w₁, w₂ of type ℚ, and I of type ORIndex N, regular N n w₁ w₂.coeff System.coeff I equals ORRegular Coeff n w₁ w₂ I.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,N\colon \mathbb{N},\;n,w₁,w₂\colon \mathbb{Q},\;I\colon \operatorname{ORIndex} N,\quad ((\operatorname{regular} N n w₁ w₂).\operatorname{coeffSystem}).\operatorname{coeff} I = \operatorname{ORRegularCoeff} n w₁ w₂ I
correspondence.leanType: theorem regular_coeffSystem_coeff (N : ℕ) (n w₁ w₂ : ℚ) (I : ORIndex N) : ((regular N n w₁ w₂).coeffSystem).coeff I = ORRegularCoeff n w₁ w₂ I

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The regular closed OR operator. In explicit terms, for n in the natural numbers, n, w₁, w₂ of type ℚ, and calconf of type calculus conf, its defining expression is regular N n w₁ w₂.to Operator Cal Conf.
correspondence.leanType: def regularOperator (N : ℕ) (n w₁ w₂ : ℚ) (CalConf : Calculus Conf) : CalConf.BiOperator

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Once the regular closed coefficients satisfy the chosen rational defect cancellation equations, the regular closed OR operator is tangential. In symbols, the proposition is the statement displayed below.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,κ\colon \mathrm{Type} _,\;\operatorname{DecidableEq} κ,\;N\colon \mathbb{N},\;n,w₁,w₂\colon \mathbb{Q},\;K\colon \operatorname{Finset} κ,\;E\colon (\operatorname{regular} N n w₁ w₂).\operatorname{ORDefectExpansion} \operatorname{CalConf} K,\;defectCoeffQ\colon \operatorname{ORIndex} N \longrightarrow \operatorname{Fin} 2 \longrightarrow κ \longrightarrow \mathbb{Q},\;hE\colon \forall (I \colon \operatorname{ORIndex} N) (\operatorname{slot} \colon \operatorname{Fin} 2) (k \colon κ), E.\operatorname{defectCoeffR} I \operatorname{slot} k = \operatorname{defectCoeffQ} I \operatorname{slot} k,\;hQ\colon (\operatorname{regular} N n w₁ w₂).\operatorname{SatisfiesRationalDefectCancellation} K \operatorname{defectCoeffQ},\quad \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{IsBiTangentialAtWeights} ((\operatorname{regular} N n w₁ w₂).\operatorname{toOperator} \operatorname{CalConf}) w₁ w₂
correspondence.leanType: theorem regular_isBiTangential_of_rational_defect_cancellation {κ : Type _} [DecidableEq κ] (N : ℕ) (n w₁ w₂ : ℚ) {K : Finset κ} (E : (regular N n w₁ w₂).ORDefectExpansion CalConf K) (defectCoeffQ : ORIndex N → Fin 2 → κ → ℚ) (hE : ∀ (I : ORIndex N) (slot : Fin 2) (k : κ), E.defectCoeffR I slot k = (defectCoeffQ I slot k : ℝ)) (hQ : (regular N n w₁ w₂).SatisfiesRationalDefectCancellation K defectCoeffQ) : CalConf.IsBiTangentialAtWeights ((regular N n w₁ w₂).toOperator CalConf) (w₁ : ℝ) (w₂ : ℝ)

Primary curved ambient source for the encoded OR recurrence.

limitations: The displayed Lean results are checked relative to structures, typeclasses, certificates, or model laws supplied as hypotheses. The module does not by itself derive every interface field from a concrete geometric backend.
